title: Chapter 37
text: Chapter Thirty - Four C aptain Cooper took charge, directing the crowd through their confusion. He sent a group of soldiers to escort the children, elders, and sickly to the forward camp, and then turned to the remaining people . “The rest of you, with me.” Cooper didn’t wait to see if they followed. He and his soldiers marched north, toward the Market. Everyone had the choice of whether to fight, but they needed to choose quickly . Most of the humans followed . A high, wailing sound echoed over Cheyenne, and Lara’s stomach sank. She’d never heard the noise before, but her body reacted to it on some primitive level. Her mind flashed back to the scene outside of Newton’s shelter — Ronin pinned on the ground as the gearheads shot him and pulled his power cell. He was facing off against those same bots now, within earshot but so far away, and she didn’t know what was happening . “You were right,” Cooper said, jarring Lara from her thoughts . “About what?” she asked. His eyes were on the shacks, on the filth and waste piled around them . “We need to help these people. I can’t… Seeing them now, how they suffered…” He shook his head and met her gaze. “How you suffered. I understand your anger .” Lara nodded. They were helping. That was more than she’d expected, when she first met the base’s inhabitants . The gate into the Market was open, as it normally was, but the activity beyond was unusual for this time of day. The rising sun cast long shadows over everything, including the cluster of bots gathered amidst the stalls . Several gearheads, touting guns, hurried through the crowd, pushing toward the Bot District. The door guard from Kitty’s clambered behind them . Cooper brought the humans to a stop and waited until the gearheads were out of sight before he proceeded forward . Greene stood at his stand, steam rising from his cooking food as though nothing unusual were happening. Some of the other bots — mostly the ones that didn’t look human — continued their normal routines, as well, seemingly unaffected by the commotion and confusion . The gate between the Market and the Bot District was open. The pair of gearheads on guard stared northeast, toward the Clinic, ignoring the bots streaming out of the District . More gunshots boomed from within the wall, each one a jolt to Lara’s heart . “Chester, stay with Newton and Lara,” Cooper ordered. “The rest of you, take up positions around the perimeter of the crowd. We’re the only defense any of these people have right now. Do not hesitate to fire on the gearheads at the gate if they make a move .” As the humans entered the Market, clumping together to form a group separate from the gathered bots, the soldiers dispersed. Lara looked over her people; they were a bedraggled bunch, malnourished and rough, most carrying makeshift weaponry — lengths of old piping, sharpened metal shards with cloth wrapped around one end, wooden planks. Gary stood at the front, bloody knife in hand . The weapons would be useless against the gearheads, but if the people were willing to fight, and if they could convince the other bots to help … Lara pointed to an empty stall. It was just a wooden board set across several stacks of cinderblocks, but it would do. “There, Newton. Get their attention .” “Not exactly conventional, but I suppose that convention is the least of our concerns in this situation…unless it is the defiance of the current — ” “I have no idea what the hell you’re talking about, but I sure as shit hope they will. Get to it .” “Right.” He walked to the stand. “Things were simpler by far when I was just a computer program .” “Stay close to me,” Chester said, positioning himself in front of Lara with his back to her and Newton . With surprising grace, Newton climbed atop the board. His shoulders sagged for a few moments before he straightened, and, finally, turned to face the crowd . “Bots, humans, synthetics, lend me your ears !” Lara arched a brow. This was on him, now. As long as he convinced the bots to fight alongside the humans, she didn’t care what he said, or how weird it sounded . Many of the bots shifted their focus to Newton, some with questioning looks on their faces — not that any of their expressions were easy to read. Except Greene’s. Blank was a simple one . “I suppose I understand how the reference would be lost on you,” Newton said to himself. He released a short burst of static, and Lara imagined that he’d cleared his throat. “I doubt that any of you know my face. I hardly know it, myself. But as I look out, I recognize the faces of every bot here . “It has been many years since I traveled this world — the Dust, as you call it — and done my work. Many years since most of you awoke …” “I know that voice,” said one of the bots, stepping forward. “But it can’t be .” “The Prophet,” another offered . Murmurs of agreement rose from the bots. Lara glanced toward the gate. The two guards had been joined by two more gearheads, all of them staring at Newton . Shit . “That is what many of you took to calling me, yes, but my name is Newton. One hundred and ninety-seven years ago, I was sent out into the wasteland, tasked with repairing and reactivating all the bots I could find. The goal was to rebuild the world, hand-in-hand with humanity .” “You saying you turned all these things on?” one of the humans demanded . They’d been sparked to anger now, had tasted blood; their volatility pulsed from them in waves . “Listen to what he has to say,” Lara shouted over the rising voices. “He’s not the one responsible for this place .” “No,” Newton said, loud but gentle. “Your anger is understandable. Justified. I blame myself in no small part for the state this world is in, Cheyenne being no exception. I hid from what I perceived as my failure for decades. Not far from here, in fact. Never truly knowing what was happening here, never knowing what all of you have been forced to endure . “Because of the courage of Lara Brooks, I chose to leave hiding. To come here and right the wrongs that have been done to all of you.” He turned his head to fully face the bots . The gearheads approached. As they drew closer, Lara recognized the one in front. He was part of the group that followed her and Ronin out of Cheyenne. The one who shot Ronin. She dropped her hand to the pistol on her thigh . “You know, regardless of your programming, that the acts carried out here are atrocities against all we were built for. You know that each and every one of us was meant for better than this .” “You need to stand down and disperse, immediately,” the gearhead in the lead said, pointing his gun toward Newton. “Deactivation is the penalty for stirring up trouble in Cheyenne. Doesn’t matter who you claim to be .” “Put your weapons down,” Captain Cooper ordered. He and several of his soldiers emerged from the anonymity of the crowd, rifles trained on the gearheads . The other three gearheads glanced at one another, expressions blank . Lara swore she saw uncertainty in their eyes . Chester’s gun was raised, too. Tense silence enveloped the crowd . “You will not receive another warning,” the gearhead said, staring at Newton . “You do not have to be the thing that he made you,” Newton replied. “You have the ability to choose. To shape your own existence .” “You don’t seem to understand.” The gearhead’s eyes finally shifted, falling directly on Lara. If he was capable of emotion, he didn’t betray any. His eyes were empty. “It’s Warlord’s will around here. He’s the reason we prosper. But you won’t be around long enough to regret this, I’m sure .” “Would he think twice before he tore you apart?” Lara moved her gaze from the lead gearhead to his companions. “The moment you cross him, he’ll have you in pieces. You’re junk to him. Scrap .” “He only ends those deserving of punishment. The troublemakers .” Everything moved with incredible slowness, and though Lara’s mind perceived it with clarity, her body wasn’t quick enough to respond. The gearhead twisted, shifting his weapon toward her. The barrel, even this far away, looked massive. She clenched the grip of the pistol, but she’d never pull it in time. He squeezed the trigger . The soldiers opened fire, their shots coming a fraction of a second after the gearhead’s. There was a high, sharp pang, and Chester swayed slightly to the side. The gearhead fell a moment later, thin wisps of smoke curling from the countless holes in him . The three gearheads who stood behind him dropped their weapons and raised their empty hands. Lara realized — stunned — that they’d also fired upon their leader. She recognized one of them, a synth with his bare-metal arm painted blue. Cobalt. He’d been the gate guard when she first entered the Bot District . The crowds murmured as the soldiers stepped forward to collect the discarded firearms . Lara pressed a shaky hand against Chester’s back. “ You okay ?” He glanced at her over his shoulder. “Fine. Chest armor deflected the round . You ?” “Thought I was dead this time. I owe you one .” “None of you have to tolerate this tyranny any further,” Newton called out over the din. “None of you have to exist under the weight of fear. Today is your opportunity to band together and fight for mutual freedom. Today is the day you can begin working toward mutual prosperity .” “After all they’ve done, why would we want to live with them?” someone shouted. Lara couldn’t tell, but she thought it was one of the humans . “Metal and meat ain’t meant to live side- by - side !” “Fuck that!” Lara yelled. She turned toward Newton, who offered his hand to help her up onto the stand. She faced the humans . “My man, a bot , is out there—” she jabbed a finger toward the bot district “—fighting for you. For us! These soldiers are bots and humans, and they come from a place where they work together, where they’re friends, where they’re family . We can live side-by-side. They’re no different than us. They feel just as deeply .” “Before this world was shattered, our kinds coexisted peacefully,” Newton said. “That was always the design. We were meant to learn from each other, to grow alongside each other, to shore up each other’s weaknesses with our respective strengths. We are not meant to be at war .” “Yet you ask us to fight?” a bot asked . “If you don’t, it will be an extermination,” Cobalt said. The crowd turned to him. “He’s not capable of stopping once it’s begun. He’s been…bored lately, so he’s taken it out on humans, and eventually he’ll kill all of them. And after this, he won’t hesitate to wipe this whole city clean and start over again .” “It’s only a matter of time before he turns to us,” another gearhead said. “He threatened to disassemble my lover if I didn’t obey. And I’ve helped him take apart some of our kind already.” His eyes found Lara’s for an instant, flashing with guilt, and pulled away . The gunfire in the bot district continued, picking up its tempo . “We must do this together. Not as bots and humans, but as a single, unified community. Gather whatever weapons we have, and march on the Clinic to finish this. There cannot be freedom without sacrifice, but you cannot allow another life, whether organic or digital, to be taken for no reason but to serve Warlord’s whims.” Newton punctuated his words by slamming his fist onto his open palm . Rapid conversation swept through the crowd, shifting to calls of agreement. Chester helped Lara down, and Newton followed behind her . Cooper approached her. “It’s time, Lara. Newton will take you to the forward camp .” “ No .” “I’m not asking, Brooks .” “It is going to be terribly, dangerous, Lara,” Newton said, settling a hand on her shoulder. “And Ronin would not be happy with any of us .” “I don’t care. As shitty as it’s been, this is my home, and these are my people. I’m not going to hide while they risk their lives for it, especially not after I asked them to fight .” “And just what the hell do you think you’re going to do in there?” Cooper demanded . “Whatever the hell I can .” He stared at her, jaw clenched. “Shit,” he muttered and ran a hand over his face. “Nancy’s going to kill me, but she gets just as stubborn, and there’s no budging her once she’s made up her mind, either. Newton, get back there. Tell them what’s happening. If they can spare any more bodies, we might need them here .” “Check on Kate,” Lara said. “ Please .” Newton’s brow-plates drew together. “Thank you , Lara .” “ For … what ?” “For getting me out of that hole, even if it was indirectly. I shall never — ” “Just save it, okay? I’ll see you soon .” Despite his lack of skin, his expression was clearly troubled. “As you say. Soon , then .” Newton walked away. She watched him for a few seconds before Cooper caught her attention . “Take this,” he said, holding out another pistol. It was larger and heavier than the one Ronin had given her. “Two hands when you shoot. It’s going to have a hell of a kick, but I don’t want you to hesitate. Just…stay back. That clear ?” Lara tightened her grip on it and nodded . Cooper pointed to Chester. “Like the Colonel said. Like a fly on shit .” “Yes, Captain .” “Good. Now let’s move. We’re already late for the party .”
